comments.body:
If you test this in podman, it's imperative to do with without `flakeretries`. Otherwise e2e failures will be invisible.

FWIW Miro Vadkerti was seeing the `cdn03` bug a week or two ago in his testing farm. There is very high confidence that the bug persists.

> Otherwise e2e failures will be invisible.

Oh okay thanks.  I'll just grep the ginkgo log files then (assuming we still collect them).

> There is very high confidence that the bug persists.

Noted.

Ugg, speak of the devil.  This flake reproduced on [the 4.4.1-rhel branch cirrus-cron run the other night](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5033884232974336).  So it's definitely still alive and kicking :cry:

### Edited/Blocked Notification

Renovate will not automatically rebase this PR, because it does not recognize the last commit author and assumes somebody else may have edited the PR.

You can manually request rebase by checking the rebase/retry box above.

 ⚠ **Warning**: custom changes will be lost.

@edsantiago any chance you've seen [this debian Error: OCI runtime error: runc: exec failed: unable to start container process: exec: "/etc": is a directory](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4722790843023360/html/int-podman-debian-13-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--Podman-exec-podman-exec-cannot-be-invoked--1)?  I didn't see it on the flake list.

It's dying on the entire debian matrix :cry:
My first guess is that `runc` changed, and is now emitting a different exit status.
Force-push: Rebased.  Small fix for Ed.  Add one test skip, removed another.
garrr...I fat-fingered my fix to Ed's date :scream:
Thanks to the kind-soul whom is re-running CI jobs :+1:
